llmWelcome:
Olá! Sou seu assistente IA ambiental. Posso ajudar a gerenciar dados de monitoramento, adicionar observações e organizar campanhas. Use o botão + Ferramentas abaixo para habilitar capacidades avançadas como análise de tendência (Mann-Kendall), amostragem ótima e auditoria ESG.

Importante: Respostas da IA podem conter erros ou alucinações. As ferramentas estatísticas (regressão, Lei de Benford) são determinísticas, mas interpretações em linguagem natural são geradas por IA. Sempre verifique os resultados com profissionais qualificados antes de tomar decisões regulatórias ou de engenharia.

temporalValues: Série temporal (valores separados por vírgula ou nova linha):
hypothesisDisclaimer: Compara observacoes pareadas entre duas campanhas de amostragem para determinar se houve mudanca estatisticamente significativa. Comumente usado para avaliar eficacia de remediacao (antes vs depois). Escolha Wilcoxon para dados nao-parametricos (recomendado para monitoramento ambiental), teste t pareado para diferencas normalmente distribuidas, ou teste de sinais para a analise direcional mais simples.
backgroundDisclaimer: Compara dados de pontos de compliance (jusante) contra dados de referencia de background (montante) para determinar se as concentracoes observadas excedem niveis naturais. Baseado na metodologia EPA Unified Guidance: calcula o percentil 95 dos dados de background e conta quantos valores de compliance o excedem. Um numero estatisticamente significativo de excedencias indica evidencia de contaminacao.
macDisclaimer: Curva de Custo Marginal de Abatimento classifica medidas por custo-efetividade (custo por unidade de reducao). Aplicavel a qualquer dominio: emissoes de carbono, eficiencia energetica, remediacao ambiental, otimizacao de monitoramento. As medidas sao ordenadas da mais barata a mais cara por unidade abatida. Valores de custo e abatimento podem ser inseridos manualmente ou vinculados a metricas do Calculator para atualizacoes dinamicas.

tourChapterBasicsDesc: Viewport 3D, painéis e famílias de elementos
tourChapterAdvancedDesc: Chaves ECO1, chat IA, blockchain, bibliotecas
tourViewportBody: Esta é a visualização 3D principal onde seu gêmeo digital ambiental é renderizado. Use o mouse para orbitar, deslocar e dar zoom.
tourCameraControlsBody: Use estes botões para dar zoom in, zoom out ou resetar a câmera para a visualização padrão.
tourRibbonBody: A barra de menu dá acesso a todas as funcionalidades. Cada aba revela uma barra de ferramentas diferente.
tourRibbonHomeBody: A aba Início contém as ferramentas mais comuns: geração de modelo aleatório, vistas e edição.
tourRibbonFileBody: A aba Arquivo gerencia operações de exportação, importação e salvamento na nuvem.
tourLeftPanelBody: Este painel mostra todos os tipos de elementos disponíveis (poços, plumas, lagos, etc). Clique em uma família para adicionar um novo elemento à cena 3D.
tourFamiliesBody: Cada família representa um tipo de elemento ambiental. Clique em uma para adicioná-la à cena.
tourAddElementBody: Clique em qualquer família de elementos na lista para adicioná-la à sua cena 3D.
tourRightPanelBody: O painel direito mostra detalhes do projeto, propriedades dos elementos e diversas abas de dados.
tourProjectTabBody: A aba Projeto mostra metadados como nome do projeto, data de criação e resumo.
tourElementsTabBody: A aba Elementos lista todos os elementos na cena com suas observações e dados.
tourBasicsCompleteBody: Você agora conhece o layout fundamental. Continue para aprender sobre gestão de dados ou explore por conta própria.
tourDataIntroBody: As abas do painel direito organizam os dados do seu projeto em categorias.
tourCampaignsTabBody: Campanhas são eventos de amostragem de campo. Cada campanha tem uma data, equipe e conjunto de observações.
tourScenesTabBody: Cenas são posições de câmera salvas. Use-as para navegar rapidamente para pontos de vista importantes.
tourAnalyticsTabBody: A aba Análises fornece estatísticas, análise de tendências (Mann-Kendall) e visualização de dados.
tourGovernanceTabBody: A aba Governança gerencia integridade de dados via blockchain e trilhas de auditoria.
tourInsertRibbonBody: A aba Inserir fornece ferramentas para adicionar elementos, observações e campanhas ao projeto.
tourModelingRibbonBody: A aba Modelagem contém ferramentas de modelagem 3D, modelagem voxel de subsuperfície e gerenciamento de furos.
tourDataCompleteBody: Você agora entende como os dados são organizados. Continue para explorar os módulos analíticos.
tourModulesIntroBody: O ecbyts inclui módulos analíticos poderosos acessíveis pela barra de ferramentas do ribbon.
tourNNBtnBody: Este botão abre o gerenciador de Redes Neurais para treinar e executar modelos de ML nos seus dados.
tourNNModalBody: Aqui você pode criar, treinar e avaliar redes neurais para previsões ambientais.
tourAerialBtnBody: Este botão abre o módulo de reconhecimento de imagem aérea/satélite para classificação via ML.
tourAerialModalBody: Envie imagens aéreas ou de satélite para classificar feições ambientais usando aprendizado de máquina.
tourCalculatorBtnBody: Este botão abre a calculadora de métricas ambientais.
tourCalculatorModalBody: Calcule métricas ambientais, conversões de unidade e limiares de conformidade.
tourInterpolationBtnBody: Este botão abre o módulo de interpolação espacial (IDW, Kriging, RBF).
tourInterpolationModalBody: Gere superfícies interpoladas a partir de observações pontuais usando diversos métodos matemáticos.
tourCrossSectionBtnBody: Visualize um perfil 2D mostrando as camadas geológicas ao longo de qualquer linha traçada no mapa.
tourCrossSectionPanelBody: Desenhe uma linha A→B no mapa para gerar um perfil geológico 2D. Exporte como PNG para relatórios.
tourSAOBtnBody: A ferramenta de matriz SAO (Segurança, Ambiente e Saúde Ocupacional) para avaliação de riscos.
tourModulesCompleteBody: Você viu os principais módulos analíticos. Continue para aprender sobre exportação e recursos avançados.
tourExportBtnBody: Exporte seu projeto como chave ECO1, JSON, CSV, GeoJSON, glTF ou formato KML.
tourAIChatBtnBody: Este botão abre o painel do assistente IA para interação em linguagem natural com seus dados.
tourAIChatPanelBody: Converse com o assistente IA para analisar dados, gerar relatórios e obter insights ambientais.
tourLibrariesRibbonBody: A aba Bibliotecas fornece acesso a bibliotecas de elementos compartilhados e configurações reutilizáveis.
tourTickerBtnBody: O ticker mostra logs de processamento em tempo real dos módulos de IA, blockchain e sensores.
tourHelpRibbonBody: A aba Ajuda contém documentação, informações sobre o produto e o botão Tour do Produto.
tourAdvancedCompleteBody: Você completou o tour completo do produto! Agora está pronto para explorar o ecbyts por conta própria.

hypothesisInterpretReject2: A diferenca entre campanhas e estatisticamente significativa (p = {{p}})
hypothesisInterpretAccept1: Nao ha evidencia de que as concentracoes mudaram significativamente
hypothesisInterpretAccept2: A variacao observada e compativel com flutuacao natural

bgCleanBullet3: Nao ha evidencia estatistica de contaminacao

bgContamBullet2: Isso e mais do que o esperado por acaso
bgContamBullet3: Ha evidencia estatistica de que esta area esta impactada

guidedTourAddElementDesc: Learn how to add a monitoring well to your 3D scene
gtAddElementIntroBody: This is the left panel where you can see element families. Each family represents a type of environmental site component.
gtAddElementFamiliesBody: Here you see the available families: wells, plumes, lakes, rivers, buildings, and more. Each creates a different 3D object.
gtAddElementClickWellBody: Click on a family item to add it to your scene. Try clicking 'Well' to add a monitoring well.
gtAddElementSee3DBody: The element now appears in the 3D viewport. You can orbit, zoom, and pan to inspect it from any angle.
gtAddElementCompleteBody: Your element is now listed in the Elements tab on the right panel. You can select it to view or edit its properties.
guidedTourEditElementDesc: Select an element and modify its name, coordinates, and attributes
gtEditElementSelectBody: Click on the Elements tab to see all elements in your model.
gtEditElementListBody: Click on any element in the list to select it. The element will be highlighted in the 3D view.
gtEditElementInspectorBody: The right panel shows the selected element's properties. You can change its name, coordinates, family, and custom attributes.
gtEditElementCompleteBody: Changes are applied immediately to the 3D scene. All edits are tracked in the undo/redo history.
guidedTourCopyPasteDesc: Duplicate elements to quickly build your site model
gtCopyPasteSelectBody: First, select the element you want to duplicate by clicking it in the element list.
gtCopyPasteCopyBody: Click the Copy button in the Home ribbon to copy the selected element to the clipboard.
gtCopyPastePasteBody: Click Paste to create a duplicate. The new element appears at a slight offset from the original.
gtCopyPasteCompleteBody: The copied element is now in your scene with a new unique ID. Repeat to quickly populate your model.
guidedTourToggleVisDesc: Show or hide elements in the 3D viewport
gtToggleVisListBody: Each element in the list has a visibility toggle. This controls whether it appears in the 3D scene.
gtToggleVisEyeBody: Click the eye icon next to an element to hide it from the 3D view. Click again to show it.
gtToggleVisCompleteBody: Hidden elements are still in your model — they're just not rendered. Useful for focusing on specific areas.
guidedTourRandomModelDesc: Auto-generate a complete sample model for testing
gtRandomModelRibbonBody: Navigate to the Home tab in the ribbon toolbar at the top of the screen.
gtRandomModelBtnBody: Click this button to auto-generate a model with random wells, observations, campaigns, and plumes.
gtRandomModelCompleteBody: Your scene now contains multiple elements with sample data. Use this to explore all features without manual data entry.
guidedTourAddObsDesc: Record a measurement (parameter + value + timestamp) on an element
gtAddObsSelectElemBody: Click on an element in the list to select it. Observations are always linked to a specific element.
gtAddObsBtnBody: Look for the 'Add Observation' button in the element panel or ribbon.
gtAddObsFormBody: Fill in the observation fields: parameter name, value, unit, and timestamp.
gtAddObsParamBody: Choose a parameter from the catalog (e.g., Benzene, pH, Water Level) or type a custom one.
gtAddObsCompleteBody: The observation is now linked to this element. It will appear in charts, reports, and analytics.
guidedTourEditObsDesc: Modify or remove existing measurement records
gtEditObsListBody: Navigate to the Elements tab and find the element whose observations you want to edit.
gtEditObsExpandBody: Click on the element to expand it and see its observations listed below.
gtEditObsActionsBody: Each observation has edit and delete buttons. Click edit to modify values, or delete to remove it.
gtEditObsCompleteBody: Changes are saved immediately. Deleted observations can be recovered with Undo (Ctrl+Z).
guidedTourBindingDesc: Link observation fields across elements for cross-referencing
gtBindingIntroBody: Bindings let you link a field from one element to another, creating references that update automatically.
gtBindingSelectBody: Click on the element that contains the observation you want to reference.
gtBindingObsBody: Choose the specific observation or field you want to bind from.
gtBindingConfigBody: Set the target element and field, plus any transforms (unit conversion, scaling, etc.).
gtBindingCompleteBody: The binding is now active. Changes to the source will propagate to the target automatically.
guidedTourDataEntryDesc: Enter multiple observations at once using the data entry matrix
gtDataEntryRibbonBody: Switch to the Insert tab in the ribbon toolbar.
gtDataEntryBtnBody: Click this button to open the bulk data entry spreadsheet.
gtDataEntryModalBody: This spreadsheet-like interface lets you enter observations for multiple elements and parameters at once.
gtDataEntryGridBody: Each row is an element, each column is a parameter. Enter values directly in the cells.
gtDataEntryCompleteBody: Click Save to commit all observations at once. Much faster than adding them one by one.
guidedTourCreateCampaignDesc: Set up a field sampling event with date, team, and scope
gtCreateCampaignTabBody: Click on the Campaigns tab in the side panel to manage your sampling campaigns.
gtCreateCampaignBtnBody: Click the Add Campaign button in the Insert ribbon to create a new campaign.
gtCreateCampaignFormBody: Fill in the campaign name, date, and team information. The campaign will organize your field observations.
gtCreateCampaignCompleteBody: Your campaign is now listed in the Campaigns tab. You can add observations to it during field work.
guidedTourPlannedReadingsDesc: Schedule future observations for an upcoming field campaign
gtPlannedReadingsTabBody: Go to the Campaigns tab to see your existing campaigns.
gtPlannedReadingsSelectBody: Click on the campaign where you want to add planned readings.
gtPlannedReadingsExpandBody: Expand the campaign to see its current readings and the option to add planned ones.
gtPlannedReadingsAddBody: Specify which elements and parameters to sample. These will appear as pending items during field work.
gtPlannedReadingsCompleteBody: Planned readings help ensure complete field coverage. They show as checkboxes during data collection.
guidedTourCampaignVisDesc: Toggle which campaign data is shown in the 3D view
gtCampaignVisTabBody: Open the Campaigns tab to see all your sampling campaigns.
gtCampaignVisToggleBody: Each campaign has a visibility toggle. Only visible campaigns contribute data to the 3D visualization.
gtCampaignVisCompleteBody: By toggling campaigns, you can compare data from different time periods or sampling events.
guidedTourManageScenesDesc: Create and switch between saved 3D snapshots
gtManageScenesTabBody: Click on the Scenes tab in the side panel to manage your saved views.
gtManageScenesAddBody: Click Add Scene to save the current camera position, visible elements, and display settings as a snapshot.
gtManageScenesListBody: Your saved scenes appear here. Each stores a complete view configuration.
gtManageScenesNavigateBody: Click on a scene to restore its camera, visibility, and settings. Great for presentations and reports.
gtManageScenesCompleteBody: Use scenes to organize different views of your model: overview, detail, cross-section, etc.
guidedTourCameraViewsDesc: Switch between isometric, orbit, and preset camera angles
gtCameraViewsControlsBody: These buttons control the camera. You can switch between preset views and manual orbit mode.
gtCameraViewsIsoBody: Click the isometric button for a standard engineering view. Great for comparing element positions.
gtCameraViewsOrbitBody: Left-click and drag to orbit. Right-click to pan. Scroll wheel to zoom. These are standard Three.js controls.
gtCameraViewsCompleteBody: You now know how to navigate the 3D viewport. Combine with scenes to save your favorite angles.
guidedTourZoomFitDesc: Zoom in, out, and fit all elements in view
gtZoomFitBtnsBody: Use the zoom buttons or mouse scroll to adjust the view distance.
gtZoomFitAllBody: Click 'Fit All' to automatically frame all elements in the viewport. Useful after adding new elements.
gtZoomFitCompleteBody: You can also double-click an element to zoom to it, or use keyboard shortcuts for precise control.
guidedTourInspectorDesc: View raw element data, coordinates, and observation details
gtInspectorRibbonBody: Look for the Inspector toggle in the View ribbon tab.
gtInspectorPanelBody: The Inspector shows raw JSON data for the selected element — coordinates, properties, and observations.
gtInspectorDataBody: This is useful for debugging, verifying data imports, and understanding the internal model structure.
gtInspectorCompleteBody: The Inspector is a power-user tool. It shows exactly what's stored in the model, unformatted.
guidedTourExportECO1Desc: Export your model as a compressed text key for sharing
gtExportRibbonBody: Switch to the File tab in the ribbon toolbar.
gtExportBtnBody: Click the Export button to open the export dialog.
gtExportModalBody: Choose ECO1 format. The model is compressed into a single text key you can copy, share, or store.
gtExportCompleteBody: Copy the ECO1 key. Anyone with ecbyts can import it to recreate your exact model.
guidedTourImportECO1Desc: Restore a model from a previously exported key
gtImportRibbonBody: Navigate to the File tab in the ribbon toolbar.
gtImportBtnBody: Click the Import button to open the import dialog.
gtImportModalBody: Paste the ECO1 key into the text area. The system will validate and decode it automatically.
gtImportCompleteBody: Your model has been fully restored with all elements, observations, campaigns, and scenes.
guidedTourSpreadsheetDesc: Import environmental data from XLSX/CSV files
gtSpreadsheetRibbonBody: Switch to the Insert tab to find the data import tools.
gtSpreadsheetBtnBody: Click this button to open the ingestion wizard.
gtSpreadsheetUploadBody: Drag and drop or browse for your XLSX/CSV file. The wizard will parse it automatically.
gtSpreadsheetMappingBody: Map your spreadsheet columns to ecbyts fields: element names, parameters, values, coordinates, dates.
gtSpreadsheetValidateBody: The wizard validates your data: checks units, formats, required fields, and flags any issues.
gtSpreadsheetPreviewBody: Review the parsed data before importing. You can fix any mapping issues here.
gtSpreadsheetCompleteBody: Your spreadsheet data is now in the model as elements and observations.
guidedTourDocImportDesc: Extract environmental data from PDF or Word documents using AI
gtDocImportRibbonBody: Go to the Insert tab to access the document import feature.
gtDocImportBtnBody: Click to open the document ingestion wizard.
gtDocImportUploadBody: Upload a PDF or DOCX file. The AI engine will analyze its content for environmental data.
gtDocImportAIBody: The AI extracts tables, measurements, and metadata from your document. Review the extracted data.
gtDocImportReviewBody: Check the AI's extraction. You can correct any misidentified values before importing.
gtDocImportCompleteBody: The extracted data has been added to your model. The original document is preserved as reference.
guidedTourDXFImportDesc: Overlay CAD drawings on your 3D environmental model
gtDXFImportRibbonBody: Navigate to the Insert tab for spatial data tools.
gtDXFImportBtnBody: Click to open the spatial blueprint import modal.
gtDXFImportModalBody: This modal handles DXF/CAD file import with coordinate system configuration.
gtDXFImportUploadBody: Select your DXF file. The parser will extract layers, polylines, and annotations.
gtDXFImportCRSBody: Set the coordinate reference system (CRS) to align the blueprint with your site's real-world position.
gtDXFImportCompleteBody: The CAD drawing is now an overlay in your 3D scene. Elements snap to the blueprint geometry.
guidedTourMapPickerDesc: Set your project location on a real-world map
gtMapPickerProjectBody: Open the Project tab in the right panel to access location settings.
gtMapPickerBtnBody: Click the map picker button to set your project's geographic coordinates.
gtMapPickerOverlayBody: Click on the map to set your site location. You can search by address or coordinates.
gtMapPickerCompleteBody: Your project now has real-world coordinates. These are used for spatial analysis and map overlays.
guidedTourTerrainLayerDesc: Generate an elevation surface from observation data
gtTerrainLayerRibbonBody: Switch to the Optimize tab for spatial analysis tools.
gtTerrainLayerBtnBody: Click to generate a terrain surface from your well elevation data.
gtTerrainLayerConfigBody: Choose the interpolation method and resolution. Higher resolution = smoother terrain but slower.
gtTerrainLayerResultBody: The terrain mesh appears in the 3D viewport. It shows the ground surface based on your data points.
gtTerrainLayerCompleteBody: The terrain layer helps visualize topography and can be combined with contamination layers.
guidedTourContamLayerDesc: Interpolate contaminant concentration across your site
gtContamLayerRibbonBody: Open the interpolation panel from the Optimize ribbon.
gtContamLayerModalBody: This modal configures spatial interpolation for contaminant concentration mapping.
gtContamLayerParamBody: Choose which chemical parameter to interpolate (e.g., Benzene, Lead, pH).
gtContamLayerMethodBody: Choose Kriging (most accurate), IDW (fastest), or RBF (smooth). Each has different trade-offs.
gtContamLayerCompleteBody: The contamination surface is now visible in 3D. Colors represent concentration levels.
guidedTourPotentiometricDesc: Visualize groundwater flow direction and hydraulic head
gtPotentiometricRibbonBody: Open the interpolation panel to generate a potentiometric surface.
gtPotentiometricModalBody: Configure the groundwater flow visualization with water level data.
gtPotentiometricWaterBody: Select the water level parameter. The system uses well water levels to compute the potentiometric surface.
gtPotentiometricFlowBody: Flow arrows show groundwater movement from high to low hydraulic head. Essential for plume migration analysis.
gtPotentiometricCompleteBody: The potentiometric map shows equipotential lines and flow vectors over your site.
guidedTourInterpMethodDesc: Configure Kriging, IDW, or RBF parameters for optimal results
guidedTourCrossSectionDesc: Crie um perfil geológico 2D a partir de superfícies interpoladas
gtCrossSectionIntroBody: Esta ferramenta cria um perfil 2D mostrando as camadas geológicas ao longo de qualquer linha traçada. Perfeita para visualização do subsolo.
gtCrossSectionOpenBody: Acesse o recurso de corte pelo painel de Interpolação.
gtCrossSectionBtnBody: Clique no botão 'Corte' com gradiente roxo para abrir o painel de corte geológico.
gtCrossSectionDrawBody: Clique em 'Desenhar Linha', depois clique no ponto A no mapa, depois no ponto B. O sistema amostrará todas as superfícies geológicas ao longo desta linha.
gtCrossSectionResultBody: O perfil gerado mostra todas as camadas geológicas como polígonos coloridos. Passe o mouse para ver elevações. A legenda mostra os tipos de solo: argila (ciano), silte (verde), areia (amarelo), cascalho (laranja), rocha (vermelho).
gtCrossSectionExportBody: Baixe seu corte como imagem PNG para relatórios e apresentações.
gtCrossSectionCompleteBody: Você criou um corte geológico. Use esta ferramenta para visualizar a estratigrafia do subsolo ao longo de qualquer transecto.
gtInterpMethodOpenBody: Access the interpolation panel from the Optimize ribbon.
gtInterpMethodMethodsBody: Three methods: Kriging (geostatistical, needs variogram), IDW (inverse distance weighting), RBF (radial basis functions).
gtInterpMethodParamsBody: Adjust parameters like power (IDW), kernel (RBF), or variogram model (Kriging) for best results.
gtInterpMethodCompleteBody: Your interpolation settings will be used for all future surface generations until changed.
guidedTourVoxelGeologyDesc: Create a 3D subsurface model with voxel blocks
gtVoxelGeologyRibbonBody: Go to the Optimize tab for 3D modeling tools.
gtVoxelGeologyBtnBody: Click to generate a 3D voxel volume from your subsurface data.
gtVoxelGeologyGenerateBody: The system creates a 3D grid of voxel blocks, each colored by lithology or contamination level.
gtVoxelGeologyToolbarBody: Use the voxel toolbar to toggle layers, change opacity, and switch between visualization modes.
gtVoxelGeologyCompleteBody: Your 3D subsurface model shows geological layers and contamination distribution in full volume.
guidedTourVoxelEditDesc: Paint and modify individual voxels in the 3D subsurface model
gtVoxelEditModeBody: Click the voxel edit toggle in the Optimize ribbon to enter voxel painting mode.
gtVoxelEditSolidBody: In solid mode, you paint entire columns of voxels at once. Good for defining geological layers.
gtVoxelEditIndividualBody: In individual mode, you paint one voxel at a time. Good for fine-tuning boundaries.
gtVoxelEditCompleteBody: Exit edit mode to return to normal navigation. Your voxel changes are preserved.
gtCreateReportTabBody: Click on the Reports tab in the right panel to start building your report.
gtCreateReportAddBody: Create a new report and give it a title. Reports are multi-section documents.
gtCreateReportEditorBody: Use the rich text editor to write your report content. You can add headings, tables, and images.
gtCreateReportSectionsBody: Add multiple sections: Introduction, Methodology, Results, Conclusions. Each section is editable independently.
gtCreateReportCompleteBody: Your report is saved and can be exported as PDF or DOCX at any time.
guidedTourSceneAnchorsDesc: Embed 3D scene snapshots directly in your reports
gtSceneAnchorsReportBody: Go to the Reports tab and select or create a report where you want to embed 3D views.
gtSceneAnchorsInsertBody: Click the insert anchor button to embed the current 3D view as a clickable reference in your report.
gtSceneAnchorsNavigateBody: Clicking a scene anchor in the report jumps to that exact 3D view — camera position, visibility, and all.
gtSceneAnchorsCompleteBody: Scene anchors make reports interactive. Readers can jump between text and the corresponding 3D view.
guidedTourExportReportDesc: Render your report as PDF or DOCX for sharing
gtExportReportTabBody: Go to the Reports tab and select the report you want to export.
gtExportReportFormatBody: Select PDF for print-ready output, or DOCX for editable documents.
gtExportReportOptionsBody: Configure page layout, margins, header/footer, and which sections to include.
gtExportReportCompleteBody: Your report has been rendered and is ready for download.
guidedTourPlumeDelineationDesc: Run the CONAMA 420 plume delineation regulatory workflow
gtPlumeDelineationRibbonBody: Click the Workflows button in the Optimize ribbon to open the workflow picker.
gtPlumeDelineationPickerBody: The picker shows available regulatory workflows. Select 'Plume Delineation' for CONAMA 420 compliance.
gtPlumeDelineationSelectBody: Click on the Plume Delineation card to start the guided workflow.
gtPlumeDelineationWizardBody: The wizard guides you through each step: parameter selection, campaign filtering, interpolation, and delineation.
gtPlumeDelineationStepsBody: Each step has instructions. The wizard validates your data and warns if prerequisites are not met.
gtPlumeDelineationCompleteBody: The contamination plume has been delineated according to CONAMA 420 thresholds.
guidedTourEmergencyResponseDesc: SAO/ESH risk assessment for emergency scenarios
gtEmergencyResponseRibbonBody: Open the Workflows button in the Optimize ribbon.
gtEmergencyResponsePickerBody: Select the 'Emergency Response' workflow for SAO/ESH risk matrix assessment.
gtEmergencyResponseSelectBody: Choose the emergency scenario: chemical spill, tailings dam, groundwater contamination, or air emission.
gtEmergencyResponseWizardBody: The wizard evaluates risk using the SAO matrix and generates an EIS integrity score.
gtEmergencyResponseCompleteBody: The emergency response assessment is complete with risk classification and recommended actions.
gtRiskAssessmentRibbonBody: Open the Workflows button in the Optimize ribbon.
gtRiskAssessmentPickerBody: Select the 'Risk Assessment' workflow for CETESB DD-38 compliance.
gtRiskAssessmentSelectBody: Choose land use type and contaminants of concern for the risk evaluation.
gtRiskAssessmentWizardBody: The wizard walks through exposure paths, validation, and compliance checking.
gtRiskAssessmentMatrixBody: Review the risk matrix showing contaminant levels vs. regulatory thresholds by land use category.
gtRiskAssessmentCompleteBody: The risk assessment report is ready with CETESB compliance status for all evaluated parameters.
guidedTourOpenAIChatDesc: Use natural language to interact with your environmental model
gtOpenAIChatRibbonBody: Look for the AI chat toggle button in the View ribbon.
gtOpenAIChatPanelBody: The chat panel opens on the side. You can type natural language commands to control the model.
gtOpenAIChatInputBody: Try typing: 'add 3 wells', 'show benzene data', or 'generate a report'. The AI understands environmental context.
gtOpenAIChatCompleteBody: The AI chat is your command center. Ask questions about your data or give instructions in plain English.
guidedTourConfigAIDesc: Set up your LLM provider and API key for AI features
gtConfigAIOpenBody: Open the AI chat panel to access provider settings.
gtConfigAIProvidersBody: ecbyts supports multiple AI providers: OpenAI, Groq, Anthropic, and local models via LM Studio.
gtConfigAIKeyBody: Enter your API key for the selected provider. Keys are stored locally and never sent to our servers.
gtConfigAIModelBody: Choose the model to use. Larger models are more capable but slower. Start with the default recommendation.
gtConfigAICompleteBody: Your AI provider is set up. You can now use natural language commands and AI-powered features.
guidedTourNLCommandsDesc: Learn the agentic command vocabulary for hands-free operation
gtNLCommandsOpenBody: Open the AI chat panel to start using natural language commands.
gtNLCommandsExamplesBody: Try: 'add a well at coordinates 50, 30', 'show all benzene observations', 'generate terrain layer'.
gtNLCommandsActionsBody: The AI can execute 769+ actions: element CRUD, interpolation, reporting, export, and more.
gtNLCommandsToolsBody: The AI has access to tools: create elements, run analysis, generate reports, and modify the model.
gtNLCommandsCompleteBody: You can now control ecbyts entirely through natural language. Type 'help' for a full command reference.
guidedTourCreateNNDesc: Define a neural network architecture for environmental prediction
gtCreateNNRibbonBody: Open the Neural Network manager from the Optimize ribbon.
gtCreateNNModalBody: This modal lets you create, train, and manage neural networks for environmental prediction.
gtCreateNNConfigBody: Set the network type, input/output parameters, and learning rate.
gtCreateNNLayersBody: Add hidden layers and set neuron counts. Start simple (2 layers, 16 neurons each) and increase if needed.
gtCreateNNCompleteBody: Your neural network architecture is defined. Next step: train it with your observation data.
guidedTourTrainNNDesc: Train your network with observation data to learn patterns
gtTrainNNOpenBody: Go to the Optimize ribbon and open the Neural Network manager.
gtTrainNNModalBody: Select the neural network you want to train from the list.
gtTrainNNDataBody: The network uses your observation data as training input. Ensure you have enough data points for reliable results.
gtTrainNNEpochsBody: Choose the number of training epochs. More epochs = better fit, but watch for overfitting.
gtTrainNNProgressBody: Watch the loss curve decrease as the network learns. Training stops when convergence is reached.
gtTrainNNCompleteBody: Your network is trained. You can now use it for prediction and what-if analysis.
guidedTourPredictionDesc: Generate predictions and what-if scenarios from trained networks
gtPredictionOpenBody: Go to the Optimize ribbon and open the Neural Network manager.
gtPredictionModalBody: Select a trained neural network to use for predictions.
gtPredictionInputBody: Enter input parameter values for the prediction. The network will estimate the output.
gtPredictionWhatIfBody: Adjust input values to see how predictions change. Great for scenario planning and sensitivity analysis.
gtPredictionCompleteBody: Review the prediction results. You can export them or use them in reports.
guidedTourEISDashboardDesc: View the EnviroTech Integrity Score radar chart
gtEISDashboardRibbonBody: Find the EIS Dashboard button in the Optimize ribbon.
gtEISDashboardModalBody: The EIS (EnviroTech Integrity Score) evaluates your model across multiple quality dimensions.
gtEISDashboardAxesBody: Six axes: Temporal coverage, Consistency, Completeness, Compliance, Accuracy, and Traceability.
gtEISDashboardRadarBody: The radar chart visualizes your score on each axis. Higher values = better data quality.
gtEISDashboardCompleteBody: Use the EIS to identify weak areas in your data collection and improve model quality.
gtRegulatoryRibbonBody: Find the Regulatory Standards button in the Optimize ribbon.
gtRegulatoryModalBody: This modal shows your observation values compared to regulatory thresholds.
gtRegulatoryThresholdsBody: Three levels: VR (reference), VP (prevention), VI (intervention). Colors indicate compliance status.
gtRegulatoryLandUseBody: Thresholds vary by land use: residential, commercial, industrial, agricultural. Select your site's use.
gtRegulatoryCompleteBody: Review the compliance report. Non-conformities are flagged with specific regulatory references.
guidedTourMannKendallDesc: Detect statistically significant trends in observation time series
gtMannKendallRibbonBody: Find the Hypothesis Test button in the Optimize ribbon.
gtMannKendallModalBody: The Mann-Kendall test detects monotonic trends in your observation data over time.
gtMannKendallConfigBody: Select the parameter and elements to analyze. The test needs at least 5 time points for reliable results.
gtMannKendallCompleteBody: Results show trend direction (increasing/decreasing), significance level, and Sen's slope estimate.
guidedTourCostAnalysisDesc: View CAPEX/OPEX breakdown and cost rollup
gtCostAnalysisRibbonBody: Find the Cost Analysis button in the Optimize ribbon.
gtCostAnalysisModalBody: The cost analysis panel shows capital and operational expenditure for your environmental project.
gtCostAnalysisCapexBody: Capital expenditure (wells, equipment, infrastructure) vs. operational costs (sampling, analysis, reporting).
gtCostAnalysisBreakdownBody: Detailed breakdown by category, element, and time period. Export to spreadsheet for financial reporting.
gtCostAnalysisCompleteBody: Use the cost dashboard to optimize your monitoring program and justify budgets.
guidedTourEVADesc: Track project progress with Earned Value Analysis
gtEVATabBody: Click the Governance tab in the right panel to access project management tools.
gtEVAPanelBody: Earned Value Analysis tracks planned vs. actual progress and costs for your environmental project.
gtEVAMetricsBody: SPI (Schedule Performance Index) and CPI (Cost Performance Index) show if you're on track and on budget.
gtEVACompleteBody: Use EVA metrics to forecast project completion date and total cost at completion.
guidedTourStoryboardDesc: Create temporal keyframes for animated model playback
gtStoryboardHudBody: The bottom panel (Constellation HUD) contains the timeline for temporal data visualization.
gtStoryboardTimelineBody: The timeline shows your observation data distributed over time. Each point is a campaign or timestamp.
gtStoryboardAddBody: Click to add a keyframe at the current time position. Keyframes define snapshots for animation.
gtStoryboardNavigateBody: Click on keyframes to jump between time positions. The 3D scene updates to show data from that moment.
gtStoryboardCompleteBody: Your keyframes define the animation sequence. Use Timeline Playback to animate through them.
guidedTourTimelineDesc: Animate your model through time to see temporal changes
gtTimelineHudBody: Open the bottom panel to access the timeline playback controls.
gtTimelineControlsBody: Use play, pause, and step buttons to animate through your temporal data sequence.
gtTimelineSpeedBody: Adjust the animation speed. Slower speeds help observe gradual changes in contamination patterns.
gtTimelineCompleteBody: Timeline playback animates your entire model over time. Great for presentations and trend visualization.
guidedTourShapeEditDesc: Draw and modify polygon boundaries for elements
gtShapeEditSelectBody: Select the element whose shape you want to modify (plume, boundary, building, etc.).
gtShapeEditRibbonBody: The edit toolbar appears with drawing and vertex manipulation tools.
gtShapeEditDrawBody: Click on the 3D viewport to add vertices. Each click adds a point to the shape boundary.
gtShapeEditVerticesBody: Drag existing vertices to reshape the boundary. Right-click to delete a vertex.
gtShapeEditCompleteBody: Click Done to save your shape edits. The element's 3D geometry updates immediately.
guidedTourLibraryDesc: Browse and download reusable environmental model templates
gtLibraryRibbonBody: Switch to the Libraries tab in the ribbon.
gtLibraryBrowseBody: Click to open the model marketplace where you can find shared templates.
gtLibraryCatalogBody: Browse available models by category: templates, reference sites, educational examples.
gtLibraryCompleteBody: Download a template to start with a pre-configured model, or share your own with the community.
guidedTourCloudDesc: Sync your model to the cloud for backup and collaboration
gtCloudRibbonBody: Go to the File tab to access cloud save and load features.
gtCloudSaveBody: Click Save to upload your model to the cloud. Requires authentication.
gtCloudAuthBody: Sign in with your account to enable cloud features. Your data is encrypted and private.
gtCloudLoadBody: Browse your cloud-saved models and load any previous version.
gtCloudCompleteBody: Your model is backed up in the cloud. You can access it from any device.

guidedTourDataLoadingDesc: Jornada completa: iniciar projeto novo, importar dados de planilhas ou shapefiles, e acompanhar completude.
gtDataLoadingHomeBody: A home mostra o score do modelo (elementos, campanhas, completude). Cada lacuna sugere uma acao — clique na seta para ir direto.
gtDataLoadingNewProjectBody: Clique em 'Novo' para comecar do zero. Se houver dados, um backup sera oferecido antes de limpar. Suas configuracoes (tema, idioma) sao preservadas.
gtDataLoadingWizardBody: Arraste uma planilha (.xlsx, .csv), um relatorio PDF/DOCX, ou um shapefile (.shp, .zip). O sistema detecta o formato e direciona ao pipeline correto.
gtDataLoadingQuickImportBody: Quando a confianca do formato e alta e nenhuma coluna precisa de revisao, ative Quick Import para ir direto a revisao. Economiza 3 cliques para arquivos bem estruturados.
gtDataLoadingMergeBody: Marque 'Mesclar com modelo existente' para adicionar dados sem apagar o que ja tem. Pocos sao pareados por ID canonico (PM-01 = PM 01). Observacoes sao deduplicadas.
gtDataLoadingCompletenessBody: Volte a home para ver o score atualizado. As sugestoes guiam: adicionar campanhas, importar coordenadas, definir limites. Repita o import ate completude 6/6.
